title: Today's Tech Brief: Google's AI-enabled mouse pointer understands 'this' and 'that'
summary: Google's AI-enabled mouse pointer, a Google security engineer arrested, and more.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Google's AI-enabled mouse pointer understands 'this' and 'that'
imageCaption: Google's AI-enabled mouse pointer understands 'this' and 'that'
imageSourceTitle: Google's AI-enabled mouse pointer understands 'this' and 'that'

body.text: I've got a couple of interesting tech stories to share with you today. First up, Google has developed an AI-enabled mouse pointer that can understand commands like 'this' and 'that'. According to The Register, this new tech might just change the way we interact with our computers. Imagine being able to tell your computer to 'delete that' or 'copy this' without having to manually select and right-click. It sounds like a small thing, but trust me, it's a game-changer.

Google's AI-enabled mouse pointer is the main event today. This new technology uses AI to understand context and intent, making it possible to use voice commands or even just think about what you want to do. No more tedious clicking and scrolling. It's like having a personal assistant built right into your computer. I'm looking at this and thinking, 'what's next?' Will we see a world where our computers can anticipate our needs?

In other news, a Google security engineer has been arrested for allegedly using confidential internal information to make trades on Polymarket. According to Wired, this engineer is accused of making over a million dollars in trades. It's a wild story that highlights the risks of insider trading and the importance of keeping sensitive information under wraps.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
